message:
fix(mcp): enforce WaEventsTool limit schema bounds at the server

Three-pass audit of crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs (pre-test
zone, lines 1-4584):

  1. unwrap/expect on tool handler hot paths: clean. Only two
     `.expect(...)` call sites outside #[cfg(test)] gates remain —
     mcp_tools.rs:297 and :305 — and both are themselves
     `#[cfg(all(test, unix))]` helpers accessing a test-only
     `cass_test_binary_override_slot` poisoned-mutex. No panicking
     unwrap / expect reaches the tool-call hot path.

  2. missing cancel wiring in async tool calls: clean. Every
     `crate::cx::for_request()` site in production code (168, 945,
     961, 1362, 1645, 1662, 1798, 2034, 2842, 2947, 3070, 3196,
     3356, 4181, 4339, 4496, 4544) is already wrapped in the
     inherit-or-fallback idiom `Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(...)`.
     Tool handlers that spin up their own `CompatRuntimeBuilder
     ::current_thread().build()` inside a sync `call()` then use the
     ambient-or-fresh Cx inside the `runtime.block_on(async { ... })`,
     so downstream storage/wezterm calls see the caller's Cx when one
     is threaded through.

  3. input validation gaps in tool schemas: defect found and fixed.

WaEventsTool's input schema at mcp_tools.rs:1599 declares
`"limit": { "type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 1000,
"default": 20 }`, but the Rust `EventsParams.limit: usize` is
deserialized via plain serde with no server-side bounds check.
Because JSON Schema validation of tool inputs is an MCP-client-side
concern and many clients don't enforce it, a caller can trivially
send:
  - `limit: 0` — schema says invalid, server accepts it and the
    downstream `EventQuery.limit: Some(0)` returns an empty page
    silently, masking client bugs;
  - `limit: u64::MAX` (or any value >> 1000) — schema says invalid,
    server accepts it and passes it straight through to
    `storage.get_events_with_cx` which in turn drives an unbounded
    `Vec::with_capacity(events.len())` allocation. A malicious or
    buggy client gets a cheap memory-pressure vector against the
    MCP server process.

Fix: add a bounds check immediately after the serde parse in
`WaEventsTool::call`. Out-of-range `limit` values now return a
structured `MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS` envelope explaining the contract
and pointing the caller at the default fallback. In-range values
(1..=1000) pass through unchanged; omitted `limit` still falls back
to serde's default_events_limit() = 20.

The other tools advertising schema bounds (WaSearchTool at :1243,
WaCassSearchTool at :504, WaCassViewTool/WaCassStatusTool
timeout_secs at :511/:621/:711) are not touched in this commit —
WaSearchTool already routes its params through
parse_unified_search_query which enforces bounds downstream; the
cass tools pass their `timeout_secs` to a subprocess exec whose
argument validation should be audited separately.

Compile-verified: `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib
--no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime` finishes
clean in 40.60s at /tmp/ft-pane6-target.
